steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The notes describe a two-stage curing process: UV light projected into a resin pool cures the object layer by layer via the dead zone mechanism, then oven baking completes the cure. The answer must capture both stages accurately.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "DLS cures 3D objects by passing through a "dead zone," adding layers to the object, then curing the object in an oven.": misidentifies what the dead zone does. ✗
  • "In DLS, UV light is projected into layers of liquid resin until the resin solidifies and passes through a "dead zone," wherein the curing is completed.": misstates when curing is complete. ✗
  • "In DLS, UV light images are projected into a liquid resin pool to cure a 3D object layer by layer; once solidified, the object is baked in an oven.": directly and accurately expresses both stages of the mechanism. ✓
  • "DLS is a form of additive manufacturing that rapidly cures liquid resin into a 3D object in which UV light solidifies layer by layer before being baked in an oven, creating a high-quality, 3D object.": buries the mechanism in awkward phrasing or adds extraneous detail. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
